ORDER TO SET ASIDE/VACATE JUDGMENT / ORDER GRANTING DEFENDANT'S MOTION TO VACATE JUDGMENT JUDGMENT OF JUL-06-2020 SET ASIDE

LAW AND MOTION DEPT. 302: DEFENDANT MELINDA NEAL'S MOTION TO VACATE JUDGMENT, AS TO THE JUDGMENT ENTERED AGAINST HER ON JULY 6 IS GRANTED. CODE OF CIVIL PROCEDURE SECTION 473(B) PROVIDES THAT "[T]HE COURT MAY, UPON ANY TERMS AS MAY BE JUST, RELIEVE A PARTY OR HIS OR HER LEGAL REPRESENTATIVE FROM A JUDGMENT, DISMISSAL, ORDER, OR OTHER PROCEEDING TAKEN AGAINST HIM OR HER THROUGH HIS OR HER MISTAKE, INADVERTENCE, SURPRISE, OR EXCUSABLE NEGLECT." WRITTEN ORDER IS SIGNED. JUDGE: ETHAN P. SCHULMAN; CLERK: J. RIOS MERIDA; REPORTER: MARIA TORREANO, CSR NO. 8600, EMAIL: MARIA.TORREANO@GMAIL.COM

calendar.matters:
MASTER CALENDAR COURT
Off Calendar Apr-23-2021 Vacated

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Jan-06-2021 Set For Trial On May-10-21 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206

Notice Of Motion And Motion Of Defendant Melinda M. Neal To Vacate Judgment
Off Calendar Dec-10-2020 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearance is made in this matter.

The Court adopts the tentative ruling.

DEFENDANT MELINDA NEAL'S Motion To Vacate Judgment, as to the judgment entered against her on July 6 is GRANTED. Code of Civil Procedure section 473(b) provides that "[t]he court may, upon any terms as may be just, relieve a party or his or her legal representative from a judgment, dismissal, order, or other proceeding taken against him or her through his or her mistake, inadvertence, surprise, or excusable neglect."

"[T]he Supreme Court has recognized that if a defendant promptly seeks relief . . . and there is no showing of prejudice, 'very slight evidence will be required to justify a court in setting aside . . . unless inexcusable neglect is clear, the policy favoring trial on the merits prevails . . . Even if the mistake were caused by some negligence on defendant's part, this negligence might be excused if it in no way prejudiced the opposing party." (Fasuyi v. Permatex, Inc. (2008) 167 Cal.App.4th 681, 696-97.) Defendant shows that the judgment was entered without her appearance at trial due a reasonable mistake of fact that the trial had been continued. Further, Plaintiff fails to show any prejudice that would result from vacating the judgment, beyond losing the advantage of the judgment and some incremental additional expense. (See Aldrich v. San Fernando Valley Lumber Co. (1985) 170 Cal.App.3d 725, 740 ["Although it might be said that there is some prejudice inherent in any protracted delay, appellants' single declaration in opposition to respondent's motion did not set forth substantial evidence of missing witnesses, evidence destroyed, and the like, to establish prejudice."].) Written order is signed.

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman; Clerk: J. Rios Merida; Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR No. 8600, email: maria.torreano@gmail.com

MASTER CALENDAR COURT
Off Calendar Jul-06-2020 Assigned To Trial Court

COURT TRIAL
Off Calendar Jul-06-2020 Judgment Entered
MINI MINUTES: This matter comes on calendar this day, having been assigned by Department 206 for Prove-Up Hearing as to defendant Melinda M. Neal, an individual.
Dept: 514. Judge: Gerardo C Sandoval. Clerk: K. Dougherty. Reporter: Giselle Casey, CSR #8098. Telephone: 415-551-3680. Email: gcasey@sftc.org.

Henry J. Yandell, Esq. appeared for plaintiff American Express National Bank, f/k/a American Express Centurion Bank.
Raquel Hernandez, Assistant Custodian of Records for plaintiff, was placed under oath and testified.
No appearance by defendant Melinda M. Neal, an individual.
Plaintiff's complaint filed for Common Counts, Book Account and Quantum Meriut was filed on August 26, 2019.
Defendant's answer to the complaint was filed on September 24, 2019.
Notice of Time and Place of Trial was served on December 30, 2019, with trial set for May 4, 2020.
Second Notice of Time and Place of Trial was served on May 5, 2020, with trial set for today, July 6, 2020.
Plaintiff's Trial Brief and Memorandum of Costs were filed on June 15, 2020.
DOE defendants were dismissed today, July 6, 2020.

After hearing the evidence presented, and good cause appearing, the Court rules in favor of plaintiff American Express National Bank, f/k/a American Express Centurion Bank, and against defendant Melinda M. Neal, an individual, as follows:

Principal/Damages - $9.965.13 (Amount pled in complaint).
Prejudgment Interest - Waived.
Attorney Fees - Waived.
Costs - $324.95.

Total - $10,290.08.

Proposed Judgment was submitted to and signed by the Court.

MASTER CALENDAR COURT
Off Calendar May-04-2020 Continued To Jul-06-20 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Dec-30-2019 Set For Trial On May-04-20 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206
